structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarm Exit Hysteresis Parameters
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Each alarm shall have an adjustable time-based hysteresis (default 5 seconds) and an adjustable percent-of-limit-based hysteresis (default 0%).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Provide adjustable time and percent hysteresis for alarm exit.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VFD Minimum Speed Synchronization
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The minimum speed shall be stored in a single software point. The active minimum speed parameter shall be read every 60 minutes via the drive's network interface. When a mismatch is detected, write the correct minimum speed to the VFD and generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Mismatch between drive's active minimum speed and software point
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Read minimum speed every 60 minutes; if mismatch, write correct value and generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The active minimum speed parameter shall be read every 60 minutes via the drive’s network interface. When a mismatch between the drive’s active minimum speed and the minimum speed stored in the software point is detected, the minimum speed stored in the software point shall be written to the VFD via the network interface to restore the active minimum speed parameter to its default value, and generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer Disable Based on High Limit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Economizer shall be disabled whenever outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high-limit setpoint as specified by local code. Setpoints shall be automatically determined based on energy standard, climate zone, and device type.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Outdoor air conditions exceed high-limit setpoint
configurable_values: High-limit setpoint (per code and climate zone)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Economizer shall be disabled whenever the outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high-limit setpoint as specified by local code. Setpoints shall be automatically determined by the control sequences (to ensure they are correct and meet code) based on energy standard, climate zone, and economizer high-limit-control device type selected by the design engineer in Section 3.1.4.3 or 3.1.6.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SystemOK FALSE Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SystemOK shall be FALSE while system is starting up or when enough components are unavailable to serve load. By default, Level 1-3 component alarms inhibit SystemOK; Level 4 does not.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: System starting up or insufficient available components
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SystemOK FALSE; operator can determine which alarms inhibit SystemOK.
configurable_values: Threshold for component unavailability (defined by engineer)
Alarm levels that inhibit SystemOK (default Level 1-3)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be FALSE while the system is starting up (i.e., before reaching setpoint) or when enough of the system’s components are unavailable (in alarm, disabled, or turned OFF) to disrupt the ability of the system to serve its load. This threshold shall be defined by the design engineer for each system. a. By default, Level 1 through Level 3 component alarms (indicating equipment failure) shall inhibit SystemOK. Level 4 component alarms (maintenance and energy efficiency alarms) shall not affect SystemOK.
